zh.Manchester Cathedral, Manchester, Lancas: 英國藍卡爾曼徹斯特曼徹斯特 凱德拉爾
zh.lifeSummary: Richard Brooke於大約1540年出生在英國藍卡爾曼徹斯特。他於1565年與Dorothy在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。他去世於1607年11月1日之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國藍卡爾曼徹斯特。

zh-hans.Manchester Cathedral, Manchester, Lancas: 英国藍卡爾曼徹斯特曼徹斯特 凱德拉爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Brooke于大约1540年出生在英国藍卡爾曼徹斯特。他于1565年与Dorothy在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是7个儿子的父母。他去世于1607年11月1日之前在他的家乡，埋葬在英国藍卡爾曼徹斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Brooke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Brook  , which preserves a trace of the Old English dative singular case, originally used after a preposition (e.g. ‘at the brook’).

History: In 1650, Robert and Mary Mainwaring Brooke brought ten children and a number of servants with them from England to MD, where Robert became governor. Although the fourteen known contemporary Brooke immigrants in VA included Robert's brothers Richard and Humphrey, the relationships of the others are unknown. Brooke family memorials remain in the Anglican church at Whitchurch, Hampshire, England.

variants.name: Brook
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a brook, stream, water-meadows or low, marshy ground, from Middle English brook, Old English brōc ‘brook, stream’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, such as Brook (Kent, Rutland) or Brooke (Norfolk).
North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a water meadow or marsh, from Low German brook (cognate with German Bruch ‘swamp’ and Old English brōc; see 1 above).
Americanized form of Dutch Broek: topographic name from broek, a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: Richard Brooke was born about 1540, in Manchester, Lancashire, England. He married Dorothy in 1565, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons. He died before 1 November 1607, in his hometown, and was buried in Manchester, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Brooke est né environ 1540, à Manchester, Lancashire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Dorothy en 1565, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils. Il est décédé avant 1 novembre 1607, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Manchester, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Brooke wurde um 1540 in Manchester, Lancashire, England geboren. Er hat 1565 in England, United Kingdom Dorothy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne. Er starb vor 1. November 1607 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Manchester, Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Brooke è nato il circa 1540 nel Manchester, Lancashire, England. Lui sposato Dorothy nel 1565, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli. Morì prima del 1 novembre 1607, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Manchester, Lancashire, England.

ja.Manchester, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアマンチェスター
ja.Manchester Cathedral, Manchester, Lancas: イギリスランカシュアイアマンチェスターマンチェスター カスエドラル
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Brookeは約1540年にイギリスランカシュアイアマンチェスターで生まれた。1565年にイギリスイギリスでDorothyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子の親であった。彼は1607年11月1日以前に故郷で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアマンチェスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Brooke(은)는 대략 1540년 영국 랭카실르 먼체스터에서 태어났다. 그는 1565년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Dorothy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1607년 11월 1일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 먼체스터에 묻혔다.

mn.about 1540–before 1 November 1607: ойролцоогоор 1540 он–1607 оны Арваннэгдүгээр сарын 1–наас өмнө

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Brooke nasceu aproximadamente 1540, em Manchester, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Dorothy em 1565, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos. Ele faleceu antes 1 de novembro de 1607, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Manchester, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Brooke родился около 1540 г. в местности манчестир, ланкашир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Dorothy в 1565 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей. Он умер перед 1 ноябрь 1607 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности манчестир, ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Brooke nació aproximadamente 1540, en Manchester, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Dorothy en 1565, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos. Murió antes de 1 de noviembre de 1607, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Manchester, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
